title: papaver rhoeas f. strigosum
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. strigosum, also known as the Poppy of the fields, is a variety of the common poppy plant (Papaver rhoeas) characterized by its distinctive hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant's flowers, seeds, and roots have been used in herbal medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The medicinal properties of Papaver rhoeas f. strigosum are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, including morphine, which have been used to treat pain, insomnia, and anxiety, while also providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of Papaver rhoeas f. strigosum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
